rag_rules: Fix AGREE type errors by resolving root causes of '<error>' types in binary expressions or named things, typically by ensuring visible identifiers or valid helpers are used.
When AGREE diagnostics reference implementation-level elements in component type annexes, move valid implementation bindings to the implementation annex or remove invalid guarantees.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure all expressions on both sides of '=>' have matching boolean types.
Only reference visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid '<error>' type diagnostics.
Move implementation-specific bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes to avoid scope mismatches.
Align helper declarations with their expression types: bool for comparisons, int for integers, real for reals.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes should only reference features visible at that scope; use implementation annexes for subcomponent paths.
anti_patterns: Do not add new package-qualified identifiers or types that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
